title: linaria × sepium subsp. camusii
intro: Linaria × sepium subsp. camusii, also known as Toadflax or Camus' Toadflax, is a hybrid perennial plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Linaria × sepium subsp. camusii include reducing inflammation, relieving symptoms of colds and flu, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its usage should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
